content_markdown:
In the circuit below, the battery has negligible internal resistance. Three identical lamps L, M and N of constant resistance are connected as shown.

The filament of lamp N breaks. Which **one** of the following shows the subsequent changes to the brightness of lamp L and lamp M?

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing a battery connected to a parallel combination of lamps L and N, which is then connected in series with lamp M.

options.label: A
options.text: Lamp L: stays the same; Lamp M: decreases

options.label: B
options.text: Lamp L: increases; Lamp M: stays the same

options.label: C
options.text: Lamp L: increases; Lamp M: decreases

options.label: D
options.text: Lamp L: decreases; Lamp M: increases

vibe_explanation: When lamp N breaks, the total resistance of the parallel section (initially $R/2$) increases to $R$. This increases the total circuit resistance, causing the total current (which flows through M) to decrease, making M dimmer. However, because the parallel section now has a higher share of the total resistance, the potential difference across lamp L increases, making L brighter.
